# Implementation Plan: Plataforma Digital Comunidad Residencial Premium

**Branch**: `main` | **Date**: 2026-03-06 | **Spec**: specs/main/spec.md
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/main/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Build a PoC mobile platform for premium residential building communities, enabling a marketplace for buying/selling between residents, service recommendations with ratings, and resident entrepreneur profiles. The mobile app uses React Native (Expo) connecting to a custom REST API (Node.js + Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL). File storage via MinIO (S3-compatible). Admin panel as a separate React web app. All backend services run in Docker Compose locally and deploy to Coolify in production.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x (strict mode)
**Primary Dependencies**: Expo SDK 52+, Expo Router, Express, Prisma, @tanstack/react-query, expo-image, expo-notifications, zod
**Storage**: PostgreSQL 16 (primary database) + MinIO (S3-compatible image storage) + AsyncStorage (offline cache)
**Testing**: Jest (unit), Supertest (API integration), Prisma with test database, Detox or Maestro (E2E)
**Target Platform**: iOS 16+, Android 13+ (API 33+), Web (admin panel only)
**Project Type**: Mobile app (React Native/Expo) + REST API (Express/Prisma) + Admin web panel + Docker Compose
**Performance Goals**: App launch < 3s, API responses < 200ms p95, image upload < 5s for 5MB
**Constraints**: Offline-capable reads (TanStack Query cache), single PostgreSQL database, Coolify deployment
**Scale/Scope**: 1 building (PoC), ~50-200 residents, ~15 screens mobile + ~5 pages admin

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Pre-Design Check

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Simplicity First (YAGNI) | PASS | PoC scope, three focused modules, Docker Compose for local dev, Coolify for deploy |
| II. Mobile-First Design | PASS | React Native (Expo) targets iOS/Android as primary; admin panel is web-only secondary surface |
| III. Resident Data Privacy | PASS | Custom JWT auth, bcrypt passwords, PostgreSQL encrypted at rest (Coolify managed), TLS in transit, private fields separated in API responses |
| IV. Offline-Resilient | PASS | TanStack Query with AsyncStorage persistence for cached reads; optimistic mutations; network status indicator |
| V. Cross-Platform Consistency | PASS | Single React Native codebase shared across iOS/Android; Expo Router for consistent navigation; one test suite for shared logic |

### Post-Design Check

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Simplicity First | PASS | No premature abstractions. Single database. Express over NestJS. Prisma enums over complex taxonomies. Docker Compose keeps local dev simple. |
| II. Mobile-First | PASS | Tab-based navigation follows platform conventions. Touch-optimized. Image compression before upload. Expo Router supports native tab styling. |
| III. Data Privacy | PASS | API never returns private fields (email, phone, unitNumber) unless authorized. bcrypt passwords. Presigned URLs for storage (no public buckets). Delete account cascades all data. Chilean Ley 19.628 noted. |
| IV. Offline-Resilient | PASS | TanStack Query persists cache to AsyncStorage. Reads work offline from cache. Optimistic updates for writes. Network indicator shows sync status. |
| V. Cross-Platform | PASS | 100% shared business logic in TypeScript. Same API client on iOS/Android. Platform-specific styling deferred to Expo defaults. |

**GATE RESULT: ALL PASS - No violations.**


**Structure Decision**: Three codebases in one monorepo: the Expo mobile app (root `app/` + `src/`), the Express API backend (`api/`), and the admin web panel (`admin/`). Docker Compose orchestrates backend services both locally and in production (Coolify). The mobile app is the primary deliverable; the API and admin panel are supporting infrastructure.
